_meta.author: Claude (national autopilot, FL state-leg batch)
_meta.note: FL state tier: Kendall (HD-18 freshman R), DiCeglie (SD-18 R), Mayfield (SD-19 R), Greco (HD-19 freshman R), Boyd (SD-20 R, Senate President-elect). Evidence from official FL Senate/House roll-call PDFs, Florida Freedom Index, FRTL ratings, campaign sites, choicetracker. TWO confirmed CROSS-PARTY GUN DEVIATIONS: Mayfield + Boyd both voted YEA on 2018 SB7026 (post-Parkland red-flag/age-21/waiting-period) -> self_defense q1 FALSE (Mayfield via official 'Y Mayfield-17' roll call; Boyd via own on-record statement). CURRENCY FIX: Mayfield won the 2025 SD-19 special; term runs through Nov 2028, NOT on the 2026 ballot. Kendall + Greco are 2024 freshmen -> NO votes exist on the pre-2025 marquee bills; scored on sponsorships/ratings/endorsements/own statements only. DiCeglie + Boyd HB1-voucher 'NO' per Freedom Index left NULL (unconfirmed; the Index's fiscal lens mislabels HB1 votes - confirmed by the Mayfield cross-check), never scored as a deviation. Unconfirmed individual roll calls left null rather than inferred from party.

profile.background: FL House District 18 (St. Johns County) since Nov 2024 (freshman; first session 2025). Former FAA air-traffic controller; small-business owner (dry cleaning); radio host. Speaker-appointed to the Florida Defense Support Commission (2025).

kim-kendall@FL.notes_set: FL State Representative, HD-18 (R), freshman elected 2024. [2026-06 national-autopilot refinement, state tier] As a 2024 freshman she has NO votes on any pre-2025 marquee bill (HB5, SB300, HB1, HB1557, SB254, HB1639, HB543, SB1718) -> those left null. Documented record: YEA on HB289 (2026 fetal wrongful-death/'unborn child' bill, House 76-34) and testified for the 6-week heartbeat bill with the Florida Family Policy Council (2023) -> pro-life. CO-PRIME SPONSOR of HB1505 (2025 Parental Rights, expanding parental consent and repealing minors' self-consent to certain care, House 80-28) -> parental-consent. Campaign supports school choice; activism for prayer at public-school graduations -> Christian-liberty/family. Endorsed by the FL Fraternal Order of Police and Sheriff Rob Hardwick; sponsored 'Trenton's Law' (DUI-manslaughter penalties) -> public justice. NO cross-party deviation. Roster note: NO evidence she held an elected St. Johns County School Board seat (prior 'District 18' confusion was the school board's own districting) -> not asserted.

0.note: YEA on HB289 (2026 fetal wrongful-death bill recognizing the 'unborn child', House 76-34); testified before a Senate committee for the SB300/HB7 6-week heartbeat ban with the Florida Family Policy Council (2023).
Campaign Issues page: supports school choice and parental authority over children's education ('reaffirms the fundamental role of parents in their children's education and healthcare decisions').

1.note: Co-prime sponsor of HB1505 (2025 Parental Rights): expands parental consent for minors' healthcare and repeals minors' self-consent to STI/family-planning/mental-health care; passed House 80-28.

0.note: Public advocacy for prayer at public-school graduations and for religious liberty 'to flourish' in public life (publicly professing faith in the public/school sphere).
Endorsed by the Florida Fraternal Order of Police (state, district & local), FL Professional Firefighters, and St. Johns County Sheriff Rob Hardwick -> affirmatively backed law enforcement.

2.note: Sponsored 'Trenton's Law' (DUI-manslaughter penalty enhancement) and HB687/HB85 (driving/boating offenses) -> proportional punishment of evildoers.

profile.background: FL Senate District 18 (Pinellas County) since 2022; FL House District 66 2018-2022. Small-business owner (family sanitation/recycling company). Former chair, Pinellas County Republican Executive Committee.

nick-diceglie@FL.notes_set: FL State Senator, SD-18 (R); FL House 2018-2022. [2026-06 national-autopilot refinement, state tier] Conservative record from the Florida Freedom Index roll-call log (lifetime 73%). Pro-life: YEA SB300 (2023 6-week ban) and HB5 (2022 15-week). YEA SB404 (2020 minor parental consent) and HB1557 (2022 Parental Rights) -> family/child sovereignty. Anti-ESG (YEA HB3 2022, HB1645) and sponsored SB1568 (2026 stablecoin pilot) -> economic stewardship. Tough-on-crime (YEA SB450 capital-jury, SB1804 death penalty for child traffickers). YEA HB1B (2021 ban on COVID vaccine mandates) -> refused federal mandate. Anti-illegal-immigration bloc (YEA SB168 2019 anti-sanctuary, SB1808 2022, SB664 2020 E-Verify). Pro-gun (YEA HB543 permitless carry, SB214 2A financial privacy; NRA-PVF endorsed 2022). NO cross-party deviation confirmed. NOTE: Freedom Index codes a 'NO' on HB1 (2023 universal vouchers); unconfirmed against the official roll call and likely a fiscal-lens artifact -> school-choice cell left null, NOT scored as a deviation. SB254/SB1718 individual roll calls not isolated -> null.

0.note: YEA on SB300 (2023 6-week 'Heartbeat Protection Act', Senate 26-13) and HB5 (2022 15-week ban).

1.note: YEA on SB404 (2020) requiring parental consent for a minor's abortion -> parental consent on minors' medical interventions.

2.note: YEA on HB1557 (2022 Parental Rights in Education) restricting classroom SOGI instruction in K-3.

0.note: Sponsored SB1568 (2026 Florida Stablecoin Pilot) allowing voluntary stablecoin payment of state fees -> supports decentralized digital currency over a state CBDC.

4.note: YEA on HB3 (2022 'Combating ESG Activism', anti-ESG) and HB1645 (anti-climate-mandate) -> opposes ESG capture.

2.note: YEA on SB450 (capital-sentence jury) and SB1804 (2025 death penalty for child-sex traffickers) -> proportional punishment of evildoers.

1.note: YEA on HB1B (2021 special session) prohibiting COVID-19 vaccine mandates -> declined an unconstitutional federal mandate.

2.note: YEA on SB168 (2019 ending sanctuary cities) and SB1808 (2022 immigration enforcement) -> opposes sanctuary policies.

3.note: YEA on SB664 (2020 expanding E-Verify) -> mandatory E-Verify for employment eligibility.

1.note: YEA on SB214 (2022 Second Amendment Financial Privacy Act, barring gun-purchase merchant codes/registries); NRA-PVF endorsed 2022 -> opposes registries/new gun restrictions.

profile.background: FL Senate District 19 (Brevard County) since June 2025 (won the SD-19 special after the FL Supreme Court ruled her term-limit-eligible). Prior: FL House (2008-2016), FL Senate SD-17 (2016-2022), SD-19 (2022-2024), FL House HD-32 (Nov 2024-June 2025). Retired banking executive (former SVP, Barnett Bank).

debbie-mayfield@FL.notes_set: FL State Senator, SD-19 (R); long House+Senate record across both chambers. [2026-06 national-autopilot refinement, state tier] CURRENCY: won the 2025 SD-19 special election (FL Supreme Court SC2025-0162 reset her term-limit clock); current term runs through Nov 2028 -> she is NOT on the 2026 ballot (candidacy_status: incumbent). Deep conservative record verified against official FL Senate roll-call PDFs: YEA SB300 (6-week ban), SB1438 (drag/adult-performance restrictions), SB254 (minor gender-care ban), HB1557 (Parental Rights), HB1 (2023 universal vouchers), SB436 (2017 student religious-liberties act), HB3 (anti-ESG), SB90 (2021 mail-ballot/drop-box + ID), SB1804 (death penalty child traffickers), HB1-B (2021 anti-COVID-mandate), SB1718 (anti-sanctuary/E-Verify), HB543 (permitless carry), SB128 (Stand Your Ground). FRTL 'A' rating. ONE confirmed CROSS-PARTY GUN DEVIATION: voted YEA on SB7026 (2018 post-Parkland red-flag/age-21/waiting-period) -> self_defense q1 FALSE (official 'Y Mayfield-17'). NOTE: Freedom Index mislabels her SB7026 and HB1 votes; the official PDFs (cited) are authoritative.

0.note: YEA on SB300 (2023 6-week ban, 26-13, 'Y Mayfield-19') and HB5 (2022 15-week); Florida Right to Life 'A' rating; on Dobbs backed overturning Roe.

2.note: YEA on SB254 (2023 ban on minor gender-affirming care, 26-13, 'Y Mayfield-19') -> affirms biological sex.

4.note: YEA on SB1438 (2023 'Protection of Children', restricting adult/drag performances near minors, 28-12) -> opposes LGBTQ promotion to minors.

0.note: YEA on HB1 (2023 universal school vouchers, 26-12, 'Y Mayfield-19') -> universal school choice.

1.note: YEA on SB404 (2020) requiring parental consent for a minor's abortion -> parental consent on minors' interventions.

2.note: YEA on HB1557 (2022 Parental Rights in Education, 'Y Mayfield-17') restricting classroom SOGI instruction.

0.note: YEA on SB436 (2017 Florida Student and School Personnel Religious Liberties Act) protecting religious expression in public schools.

4.note: YEA on HB3 (2023 'Combating ESG Activism', anti-ESG) and HB1645 (removing climate-change mandates) -> opposes ESG capture.

1.note: YEA on SB90 (2021, 23-17, 'Y Mayfield-17') which tightened ID/signature-match requirements.

3.note: SB90 (which she voted for) restricted drop boxes and limited third-party ballot collection -> opposes mass drop-box/harvesting.

2.note: YEA on SB1804 (2025 death penalty for child-sex traffickers) and SB450 (8-4 jury for death sentences) -> proportional punishment of evildoers.

1.note: YEA on HB1-B (2021 special session, 24-14, 'Y Mayfield-17') prohibiting COVID-19 vaccine mandates -> declined an unconstitutional federal mandate.

2.note: YEA on SB1718 (2023 anti-sanctuary, 'Y Mayfield-19'), SB168 (2019 ban on sanctuary cities), SB1808 (2022) -> opposes sanctuary policies.

3.note: YEA on SB664 (2020 E-Verify expansion) and SB1718 (E-Verify provisions) -> mandatory E-Verify.

0.note: YEA on HB543 (2023 permitless/constitutional carry, 'Y Mayfield-19') and SB214 (2A Financial Privacy Act).

1.note: CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: voted YEA on SB7026 (2018 Marjory Stoneman Douglas Act, 20-18, official 'Y Mayfield-17') creating FL red-flag/risk-protection orders + age-21 long-gun purchase + 3-day waiting period + bump-stock ban -> a recorded vote FOR red-flag/gun restrictions.

3.note: YEA on SB128 (2017 strengthening Stand Your Ground, shifting the burden to the prosecution) and SB1052 (2021 right to self-defense).

profile.background: FL House District 19 (Flagler County + part of St. Johns) since Nov 2024 (freshman; won the open seat vacated by termed-out Speaker Paul Renner). Attorney (St. Augustine); U.S. Navy JAG Corps lieutenant 2019-2024; Georgetown JD.

sam-greco@FL.notes_set: FL State Representative, HD-19 (R), freshman elected 2024. [2026-06 national-autopilot refinement, state tier] As a 2024 freshman he has NO votes on any pre-2025 marquee bill (SB300, HB1, SB254, HB1639, HB543, SB1718) -> those left null. Strongest documented dimension is LIFE: Florida Right to Life 'A' rating (2024) PLUS prime sponsor of HB1517 (wrongful death of an unborn child), HB7025 (parental consent before terminating a pregnancy), and HB289 (2026 fetal wrongful-death, prime sponsor + YEA, House 76-34). Strong public-justice record: Florida PBA + sheriff endorsements ('back the blue'); sponsored 'Missy's Law' (HB445, signed) holding those found guilty of dangerous crimes without bond until sentencing and expanding the dangerous-crime list to child-exploitation offenses; HB1515 criminal-justice accountability. NRA-PVF endorsed (2024) -> pro-gun. NO cross-party deviation. Marriage/sexuality, Christian-liberty, economic, election, federal-overreach, and border specifics returned no clear citable position -> null.

0.note: Florida Right to Life 'A' rating (2024); prime sponsor of HB1517 (wrongful death of an unborn child) and HB289 (2026 fetal wrongful-death, prime sponsor + YEA, House 76-34) -> affirms personhood of the unborn.

1.note: Prime sponsor of HB7025 (2025 parental consent requirements before a minor terminates a pregnancy) -> parental consent on minors' medical interventions.

0.note: Endorsed by the Florida Police Benevolent Association and Sheriffs Staly (Flagler) & Hardwick (St. Johns); campaigned to 'back the blue' -> affirmatively backed law enforcement.

1.note: Sponsored 'Missy's Law' (HB445, signed) denying bond to those found guilty of dangerous crimes until sentencing -> anti-soft-on-crime/anti-cashless-release direction.

2.note: Missy's Law expanded the dangerous-crime list to include child-pornography/exploitation offenses; also sponsored HB1515 (criminal-justice accountability) -> proportional punishment of evildoers.

1.note: NRA-PVF endorsed (2024 cycle); self-described pro-Second Amendment -> opposes new gun restrictions/registries.

profile.background: FL Senate District 20 (Manatee County) since 2020 (SD-21 2020-2022); designated Senate President-elect for 2026-28; Senate Majority Leader 2024-2026. FL House 2010-2018. Insurance agency owner; chaired Senate Banking & Insurance. Former Palmetto City Council/Mayor.

jim-boyd@FL.notes_set: FL State Senator, SD-20 (R), Senate President-elect for 2026-28; FL House 2010-2018. [2026-06 national-autopilot refinement, state tier] Confirmed marquee votes from official FL Senate roll-call PDFs: YEA SB300 (6-week ban), SB254 (minor gender-care ban), HB1557 (Parental Rights), SB1718 (anti-sanctuary/E-Verify), HB543 (permitless carry); YEA SB1804 (death penalty child traffickers). ONE confirmed CROSS-PARTY GUN DEVIATION: voted YEA on SB7026 (2018 post-Parkland age-21/red-flag package) -> self_defense q1 FALSE; this rests on his OWN on-record statement (Oct 2025) admitting he 'voted for the Parkland bill that raised the age to 21,' and as of late 2025 he remained noncommittal on repealing it. NOTE: Freedom Index codes a 'NO' on HB1 (vouchers) and on the FY24-25 budget; these are fiscal-lens dings, unconfirmed against official roll calls and not ideological reversals -> school-choice and economic cells left null rather than scored as deviations. HB3/SB7054 (anti-ESG/anti-CBDC) individual votes not isolated -> null.

0.note: YEA on SB300 (2023 6-week ban, 26-13); on record: 'I believe in the sanctity of life... one of our greatest charges is to protect life.'

2.note: YEA on SB254 (2023 ban on minor gender-affirming care, 26-13) -> affirms biological sex.
YEA on HB1557 (2022 Parental Rights in Education, 22-17) restricting classroom SOGI instruction.
YEA on SB1804 (death penalty for child-sex traffickers) -> proportional punishment of evildoers.
YEA on SB1718 (2023 anti-sanctuary/illegal-immigration crackdown, 27-10) -> opposes sanctuary policies.

3.note: SB1718 (which he voted for) expanded mandatory E-Verify employment-eligibility requirements.

1.note: CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: by his own on-record statement (Oct 2025) he voted FOR the 2018 SB7026 Parkland package that raised the long-gun purchase age to 21 and created FL's red-flag/risk-protection orders -> a recorded vote FOR gun restrictions; as of late 2025 he remained noncommittal on repeal.
